governingInstruments.name: Bank Secrecy Act (BSA), 31 U.S.C. §§ 5311-5336
platformImplications: SAR existence is absolutely confidential: 31 U.S.C. § 5318(g)
No SAR data or SAR-derived signals may be published on platform
AML programs must include ongoing transaction monitoring
Signals must avoid language that implies existence of a SAR

governingInstruments.name: Anti-Money Laundering Act of 2020
governingInstruments.status: Partially implemented; FinCEN rulemaking ongoing (April 2026 proposed rule)
platformImplications: Risk-based prioritisation of AML signals required
Effectiveness assessment of information sharing programs required
Innovation provisions: allow FinCEN Innovation Hours for platform piloting

governingInstruments.name: USA PATRIOT Act § 314(a)
platformImplications: Mandatory response to FinCEN 314(a) requests
Platform must support emergency override for 314(a) lookups
Not relevant to voluntary cross-border platform sharing

governingInstruments.name: USA PATRIOT Act § 314(b)
platformImplications: Platform participation requires annual FinCEN 314(b) registration for each US FI
Information sharing permitted only for AML/CFT purposes
SAR existence still cannot be shared under 314(b)
Safe harbour applies to registered institutions sharing for permissible purposes
Domestic-only: no international 314(b) safe harbour
Cross-border sharing of 314(b) intelligence requires MLAT or bilateral FIU channel

governingInstruments.name: Electronic Fund Transfer Act (EFTA) and Regulation E (12 CFR Part 1005)
platformImplications: Unauthorised EFT signals: flag Reg E coverage (consumer accounts, debit, ACH, P2P)
Signal must include T+2_day and T+60_day liability cutoff dates
APP/authorised fraud not covered by Reg E: no mandatory reimbursement obligation
Wire transfers and credit cards not covered

governingInstruments.name: Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act (GLBA) / FTC Safeguards Rule 16 CFR Part 314 (updated 2023)
platformImplications: NPI may only be shared with service providers under contract
No federal cross-border transfer restriction for NPI
State law (CCPA, CT, MT) may impose additional obligations
Contractual safeguards required with platform as data processor

governingInstruments.name: CFPB Section 1033 Open Banking Rule (October 2024)
platformImplications: Transaction and account data portability obligations may create new signal sources
Platform must not use 1033 data access for AML purposes without separate authorisation
Tokenised payment initiation data cannot be used to restrict competition

governingInstruments.name: NIST SP 800-188: De-Identifying Government Datasets (September 2023)
platformImplications: Federal baseline for de-identification
Distinguishes direct identifiers from quasi-identifiers
Recommends HMAC (FIPS 198-1) for pseudonymisation
K-anonymity and differential privacy recognised
FF1 FPE (NIST SP 800-38G Rev.1, 2025 draft) recognised; FF3 deprecated

appFraudReimbursementCap.note: No mandatory APP/authorised fraud reimbursement regime in the US. Regulation E covers unauthorised transactions only.

dataProtection.framework: GLBA / FTC Safeguards Rule 2023 / NIST SP 800-188 / State law patchwork

quasiIdentifiers.note: IP address and device fingerprint are personal data under CCPA; federal treatment is less clear
